subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Tangent Lines & Linearization
stimulus:
Let f be the continuous function defined on (cid:62)(cid:16)4,3(cid:64) whose graph, consisting of three line segments and a
x
semicircle centered at the origin, is given above. Let g be the function given by g(cid:11)x(cid:12) (cid:32) (cid:212) f(cid:11)t(cid:12) dt.

parts.prompt: Find the values of g(cid:11)2(cid:12) and g(cid:11)(cid:16)2(cid:12).

scoring_points.description: Correct value of g(2) using integral
Correct value of g(−2) using integral and graph area

parts.prompt: For each of g(cid:132)(cid:11)(cid:16)3(cid:12) and g(cid:132)(cid:132)(cid:11)(cid:16)3(cid:12), find the value or state that it does not exist.

scoring_points.description: g''(−3) does not exist — f has a corner at x = −3

required_elements: $g''(-3) = f'(-3)$ does not exist because f has a corner/cusp at $x = -3$

common_traps: Stating g''(-3) exists when the graph has a corner at x = -3

parts.prompt:
Find the x-coordinate of each point at which the graph of g has a horizontal tangent line. For each of these
points, determine whether g has a relative minimum, relative maximum, or neither a minimum nor a
maximum at the point. Justify your answers.

scoring_points.description: Correct horizontal tangent x-values where g'(x) = f(x) = 0
Correct classification: relative min at x = −1, neither at x = 3

required_elements: $f$ changes from – to + at $x = -1$: relative minimum
$f$ does not change sign at $x = 3$: neither extremum

common_traps: Concluding relative max at x = 3 without sign analysis

scoring_points.description: Valid justification for each classification via sign of f

required_elements: Sign analysis of f near each critical point

parts.prompt:
For (cid:16)4 (cid:31) x (cid:31) 3, find all values of x for which the graph of g has a point of inflection. Explain your
reasoning.

scoring_points.description: Correct inflection points x = −2 and x = 1 with explanation
Correct reasoning: g'' = f' changes sign at inflection points

required_elements: Explains that $g''(x) = f'(x)$ changes sign at the identified x-values
